summary: Ukrainian drones are now destroying Russian vehicles at distances exceeding 40 km from the front line, with loitering munitions targeting equipment within a 100 km radius, combining cheap mass-produced FPVs with expensive foreign models. Belarus has deployed armored vehicles like the MZKT-233036-011 "Lis" and MZKT-490100-217 with anti-drone protection, while Russian forces are using the "Elka" anti-aircraft drone to kinetically intercept Ukrainian UAVs, including the "Hornet," at ranges up to 1,300 meters. The enemy is reducing FPV control frequency and increasing REB antenna sizes, while ZALA Z-16 reconnaissance drones with onboard AI are accelerating target detection and strike times. Russian operations like "Rubicon" have destroyed 116 Ukrainian UAVs and control points, and FAB-3000 bombs with UMPK kits are targeting Ukrainian UAV launch sites. Ukrainian forces are training new recruits with psychological obstacle courses and countering Russian FPV attacks using drone detectors, while Russian soldiers attempting to surrender have been killed by FPV drones.
